data.description:

Linkers Industries Limited Class A ( (LNKS) ) has provided an announcement.

Linkers Industries Limited, a foreign private issuer listed in the U.S., has reported unaudited interim condensed consolidated financial statements for the six months ended December 31, 2025, covering its Malaysian operations. The company’s filing on April 29, 2026 details modest revenue of RM10.99 million and gross profit of RM1.23 million, alongside a solid equity base of RM34.63 million and reduced liabilities, offering investors greater visibility into its balance sheet strength and mid‑fiscal‑year performance.

Total assets declined to RM43.01 million from RM48.40 million at June 30, 2025, with cash, inventories and receivables all lower, while accumulated losses widened to RM2.99 million despite stable share capital and share premium. The publication of these unaudited figures, including XBRL-formatted data for regulatory compliance, helps stakeholders assess Linkers Industries’ financial health and risk profile at the half‑year mark as it navigated operating costs and investment in an associate during the period.

More about Linkers Industries Limited Class A

Linkers Industries Limited is a Malaysia-based company listed in the United States as a foreign private issuer. The group operates through manufacturing assets in Kedah, Malaysia, and reports its results in Malaysian ringgit with U.S. dollar translations for investors in international capital markets.

Average Trading Volume: 784,693

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $2.75M

data.title: Galapagos Shareholders Approve Name Change to Lakefront Biotherapeutics and Broad Governance Overhaul
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Galapagos ( (GLPG) ) has provided an announcement.

On 28 April 2026, Galapagos NV held its Annual and Extraordinary Shareholders’ Meetings, where investors approved the 2025 annual and remuneration reports, discharged directors and the auditor from liability, and confirmed a broad refresh of the board with the appointment of CEO Henry Gosebruch as executive director and several new independent and non-executive directors. Shareholders also reappointed the statutory auditor and mandated it to provide assurance on the company’s CSRD sustainability reporting, underlining an increased focus on governance and non-financial disclosure.

At the Extraordinary Meeting the company’s owners backed a name change to “Lakefront Biotherapeutics,” effective 8 May 2026, authorized the board to repurchase shares, and renewed authorized capital by up to 20% of existing share capital, giving management more financial and strategic flexibility. The governance overhaul and new capital tools are intended to support Galapagos’ ongoing transformation and next growth phase, with incoming chair Gino Santini expected to play a key role in executing the strategy and balancing patient impact with sustainable shareholder returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (GLPG) stock is a Hold with a $28.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Galapagos stock, see the GLPG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GLPG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GLPG is a Neutral.

GLPG scores strongest on financial positioning (very low leverage and large cash resources) and supported valuation (low P/E). The main offset is persistent negative operating/free cash flow and earnings quality concerns highlighted by non-recurring profit drivers and ongoing restructuring/wind-down cash needs; technical signals are broadly neutral.

To see Spark’s full report on GLPG stock, click here.

More about Galapagos

Galapagos NV, soon to be renamed Lakefront Biotherapeutics, is a Belgium-based biotechnology company focused on developing meaningful medicines for patients with serious diseases in areas of high unmet need. Leveraging a modality-agnostic asset selection strategy and strong deal-making capabilities, it prioritizes oncology and immunology & inflammation programs with clear clinical proof-of-concept to create value for patients and shareholders.

GCL Global Holdings ( (GCL) ) has issued an update.

In April 2026, GCL Global Holdings Ltd. partially redeemed approximately $3.18 million of its outstanding senior convertible notes originally issued under a May 21, 2025 Securities Purchase Agreement, and exchanged the remaining balance for a warrant to purchase 1,125,000 ordinary shares at an initial exercise price of $8.00 per share. The amendment and exchange eliminated the prospect of additional note issuances under the prior financing, simplifying GCL’s capital structure and shifting the investor’s remaining exposure from debt to equity-linked securities.

On April 29, 2026, GCL announced that its publishing subsidiary 4Divinity entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with Chengdu Cangmo for proposed worldwide publishing rights to the Ming Dynasty–set action-stealth RPG “A Whisper of Fall: Jinyiwei.” The prospective deal, which would cover PC, console and mobile platforms and target a 2027 launch on PlayStation 5 and PC, underscores GCL’s push to elevate Chinese-developed AA titles globally and deepen its pipeline of culturally distinctive Asian gaming IP, potentially strengthening its competitive position in international publishing.

The score is held back primarily by weak cash flow and rising leverage despite improving revenue and a return to profitability. data.title: Braskem Calls Fully Digital Shareholder Meeting to Overhaul Bylaws and Governance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Braskem SA ( (BAK) ) has provided an announcement.

Braskem S.A. has called an extraordinary general meeting to be held digitally on May 28, 2026, as it advances a shareholding transaction disclosed in material facts dated April 20 and 23. The company plans broad amendments to its bylaws to align with a new shareholders’ agreement, refine corporate governance rules for the general meeting, Board of Directors and Executive Board, and formally embed several key board committees, including finance, strategy, people and safety, into its statutes.

Shareholders will also vote on consolidating the restated bylaws, authorizing an early end to the current Executive Board term so its new two-year mandate coincides with the Board elected at the April 29, 2026 AGM, electing a new Board of Directors, and replacing members of the Fiscal Council. The meeting will be held exclusively via Webex, with participation allowed either through remote voting ballots or direct access to the digital platform, and shareholders must submit ownership and identification documents in advance under Brazil’s CVM Resolution 81, underscoring the company’s shift toward fully digital, procedure-heavy governance.

The most recent analyst rating on (BAK) stock is a Sell with a $1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Braskem SA stock, see the BAK Stock Forecast page.

Technical indicators also point to soft near-term momentum. data.description:

Frontier Nuclear and Minerals ( (FNUC) ) has issued an update.

On April 29, 2026, Frontier Nuclear and Minerals said its portfolio company Kadmos Energy Services launched a dual experimental validation program to underpin its small modular reactor design and U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission licensing strategy. The effort, built around two purpose-built test beds for thermal-hydraulic performance and containment safety, is intended to generate high‑fidelity data that de-risks licensing, accelerates development timelines and supports Kadmos’ push into data center, industrial and defense power markets, potentially enhancing value for Frontier’s investors and strengthening its position in next‑generation nuclear power.

Kadmos’ SMR concept uses a below‑grade pressurized water reactor with passive safety features and leverages proven light‑water technology and existing supply chains to reduce technological and regulatory risk. By advancing engineering, testing and licensing in parallel and targeting initial commercial operations in the early 2030s, Kadmos is seeking a capital‑efficient path to deployment that could differentiate it from rival SMR developers and reinforce Frontier Nuclear’s broader nuclear fuel cycle strategy.

More about Frontier Nuclear and Minerals

Frontier Nuclear and Minerals Inc., doing business as Frontier Nuclear, is a nuclear fuel cycle company focused on building a U.S.-based platform through uranium exploration and development, alongside targeted investments in enrichment and reactor technologies. The company aims to position itself across the nuclear fuel value chain, evaluating opportunities that align with its long-term strategy to supply secure, carbon-free energy solutions.

data.description:

Akanda Corp ( (AKAN) ) has issued an update.

Akanda Corp. convened a special meeting of shareholders in Toronto on April 27, 2026, but was forced to adjourn without conducting any business because the required quorum was not met. The Nasdaq-listed company will reconvene the meeting on May 25, 2026, keeping the same record date of February 26, 2026, so only shareholders of record on that date remain eligible to vote.

The adjournment underscores ongoing shareholder engagement challenges, with the company using the extra month to solicit additional proxies to reach the minimum threshold of two shareholders representing at least 10% of outstanding voting shares. Previously submitted proxies will carry over to the reconvened meeting unless revoked, and Akanda is urging investors to vote promptly to avoid further delays in its corporate decision-making.

More about Akanda Corp

Akanda Corp. is a Canada-based company that, through cannabis-focused subsidiaries, cultivates and distributes high-quality cannabis and wellness products with a global consumer focus. It also controls First Towers & Fiber Corp., an emerging Mexican telecommunications infrastructure developer specializing in cellular towers and dark fiber networks for national carriers and technology providers.

First Towers & Fiber aims to deliver scalable, reliable connectivity solutions that support commercial expansion and digital inclusion in Mexico. Akanda positions itself at the intersection of regulated cannabis markets and digital infrastructure growth, targeting both health-oriented consumers and telecom partners in developing markets.

data.title: Chiesi to Acquire KalVista Pharmaceuticals in Cash Merger
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

KalVista Pharmaceuticals ( (KALV) ) has issued an update.

On April 29, 2026, KalVista Pharmaceuticals and Italy’s Chiesi Group signed a definitive merger agreement under which Chiesi will acquire KalVista via a tender offer at $27.00 per share in cash, valuing the equity at about $1.9 billion and representing a 36% premium to KalVista’s 30‑day VWAP. The transaction, unanimously approved by both boards and not subject to a financing condition, is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026 subject to a majority tender of shares and antitrust and other customary regulatory approvals.

Following completion, KalVista will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Chiesi, which will assume responsibility for EKTERLY (sebetralstat), the first oral, on‑demand therapy for hereditary angioedema, already showing strong early uptake with $49 million in 2025 sales. The deal marks Chiesi’s largest acquisition to date and is intended to significantly expand its rare immunology franchise, U.S. commercial footprint and progress toward its 2030 revenue target, while delivering an immediate all‑cash exit for KalVista shareholders and potentially accelerating global patient access to sebetralstat.

The most recent analyst rating on (KALV) stock is a Buy with a $36.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KalVista Pharmaceuticals stock, see the KALV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on KALV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KALV is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial fundamentals (large losses, heavy cash burn, and a strained balance sheet with negative equity), despite strong technical momentum and a positive corporate update indicating accelerating product uptake and expanding commercialization.

To see Spark’s full report on KALV stock, click here.

More about KalVista Pharmaceuticals

KalVista Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering and developing innovative therapies for rare diseases, with a key emphasis on hereditary angioedema (HAE). Its lead product, EKTERLY (sebetralstat), is a novel oral, on‑demand plasma kallikrein inhibitor for treatment of acute HAE attacks in patients aged 12 and older, commercially launched in major markets including the U.S., U.K., EU and Japan.

The latest update is out from Scotts Miracle-Gro Company ( (SMG) ).

The Scotts Miracle-Gro Company, a leading North American marketer of branded consumer lawn and garden products under the Scotts, Miracle-Gro, Ortho and Tomcat brands, reported strong financial results for its second quarter ended March 28, 2026. The consumer-focused business, which generates about $3.3 billion in annual sales, sells primarily through major retailers and benefits from its established market share in lawn care, gardening and pest control.

For the quarter, net sales rose 5% to $1.46 billion, while GAAP and non-GAAP gross margin improved by 280 and 240 basis points, respectively, lifting adjusted EBITDA by 9% and boosting GAAP earnings from continuing operations to $4.46 per share. Management highlighted improved net leverage of 3.71x, down from 4.41x a year earlier, and reaffirmed its fiscal 2026 outlook, signaling continued focus on margin expansion, modest U.S. consumer sales growth, stronger free cash flow and capacity for shareholder-friendly actions such as its multi-year share repurchase program.

data.title: Daqo New Energy Swings to Deep Q1 2026 Loss as Polysilicon Sales Slump Amid Market Glut
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Daqo New Energy ( (DQ) ) is now available.

Daqo New Energy reported unaudited first-quarter 2026 results on April 29, 2026, showing polysilicon production of 43,402 MT, above guidance, but sales volumes plunged to 4,482 MT as the company avoided selling below cost amid weak prices. Revenue dropped to $26.7 million from $221.7 million in the prior quarter, driving a gross loss of $139.4 million, a net loss attributable to shareholders of $88.4 million, and sharply negative margins.

Management cited seasonal softness, high inventories, rising module input costs, and geopolitical tensions as factors depressing solar demand and intensifying industry overcapacity, which pushed polysilicon prices below production costs. In response, Daqo ran at roughly 57% capacity utilization, emphasized financial discipline, and highlighted its $2.0 billion pool of cash and liquid assets and zero debt as key strengths, while guiding second-quarter 2026 polysilicon output to 35,000–40,000 MT and full-year 2026 production to 140,000–170,000 MT.

The score is driven primarily by mixed fundamentals: a very strong, low-debt balance sheet and improving operations, but profitability and free cash flow remain negative. Daqo New Energy Corp. is a China-based manufacturer of high-purity polysilicon used in the global solar photovoltaic industry. LakeShore Biopharma ( (LSBCF) ) has provided an update.

On April 29, 2026, LakeShore Biopharma announced it had entered into an amended merger agreement with Oceanpine Skyline Inc. and Oceanpine Merger Sub Inc., revising a going-private deal first signed in November 2025. Under the revised terms, Oceanpine’s Merger Sub will merge into LakeShore Biopharma in a transaction implying an equity value of about US$2.7 million, with the company to become a wholly owned subsidiary of Oceanpine Skyline and to delist from the OTC Pink Open Market if the deal closes.

The amendment sharply cuts the per-share merger consideration from US$0.90 to US$0.066, though the new price still represents a roughly 46.7% premium to the March 24, 2026 closing price and a 23.3% premium to the 10-day volume-weighted average up to that date. The revised agreement also extends the termination date by nine months and lowers termination fees, while securing funding through Oceanpine Capital’s cash contribution and equity rollovers, and it is backed by a shareholder group controlling about 53.35% of voting rights, with closing targeted for the third quarter of 2026 subject to customary approvals.

First Mining Gold ( (TSE:FF) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 29, 2026, First Mining Gold reported that new joint-venture partner Bellavista Resources completed the acquisition of FireFly Metals’ interest in PC Gold Inc., which holds the Pickle Crow Gold Project. Bellavista also paid C$3 million to exercise its buy-down right, reducing First Mining’s stake in PC Gold from 30% to 20%, while leaving First Mining free carried to a decision to mine at Pickle Crow.

The update brings in a partner led by executives with a track record at De Grey Mining, which was sold for US$3.3 billion in May 2025, potentially strengthening the development outlook for Pickle Crow. For First Mining, the transaction crystallizes cash, maintains exposure to the project’s upside without near-term funding obligations, and allows continued focus on de-risking its flagship Springpole and Duparquet projects in Canada.

data.title: Top Wealth Group Delays Annual 20-F Filing
data.description:
Top Wealth Group Holding Limited ( (TWG) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Top Wealth Group Holding Limited has notified investors that it will be late in filing its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025, via a Form 12b-25. The company emphasizes that the delay relates specifically to this Form 20-F (Yearly Report) and not to any other periodic filings.

Management says it needs additional time to complete its review of the annual financial statements before finalizing the Form 20-F (Yearly Report). The filing suggests this is mainly a timing and review issue, with no mention of auditor changes, internal control breakdowns, or special committee investigations.

Top Wealth Group states that it expects to submit the delayed Form 20-F (Yearly Report) no later than the fifteenth calendar day after the original SEC deadline. This aligns with the standard extension window provided under Form 12b-25 rules for yearly filings.

The company indicates it does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations versus the prior fiscal year to be reflected in the upcoming earnings statements. As with all such notifications, this assurance still carries an element of forward-looking uncertainty until the full Form 20-F (Yearly Report) is publicly filed.

The registrant confirms that all other required SEC reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, signaling ongoing efforts to remain in compliance. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of the Board, Kim Kwan Kings Wong, dated April 29, 2026.

data.title: Micropolis AI Robotics Delays Annual 20-F Filing
data.description:
Micropolis Holding Company ( (MCRP) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Micropolis AI Robotics has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Annual Report on Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company says the late filing relates specifically to the completion of its year-end audit and not to any broader reporting failures.

The main reason for the delay is that the audit of its financial statements has not yet been finalized and could not be completed without what management describes as unreasonable effort or expense. Micropolis AI Robotics notes it is working closely with its external auditors to finish the required procedures and close its books.

The company currently expects to file the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) no later than the fifteenth calendar day after the original due date, which is the maximum extension period allowed under this type of SEC notice. Management emphasizes that the goal is to submit the annual report as soon as it is practically possible within that window.

Micropolis AI Robotics does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year based on information available today. However, it reminds investors that all statements about timing and potential financial outcomes are forward-looking and subject to change as the audit and closing process continues.

The company underscores that it has remained current on all other required SEC filings over the past 12 months, signaling an ongoing commitment to regulatory compliance. The notification was signed on April 29, 2026, by Chief Executive Officer Fareed Aljawhari, who formally authorized the filing on behalf of the company.
